summary: Round 2 verification: both targeted round-1 findings (#1 null-exit branch coverage, #6 Locale import hygiene) are resolved correctly in commit 405e382, and findings #2/#3/#4/#5 remain as documented deferrals with no drift. The supplementary test-reviewer #1 anchor fix for `crashLogHeadTruncation_preservesHeadAndMarks` is also applied correctly — the new `endsWith("[truncated at 100 KB]")` assertion mirrors the head-anchored logcat-tail symmetry and would fail a regression that misplaces the marker. No new regressions. Grep invariants hold: `ErrorLog|errorLog` in `app/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/logexport/` → single KDoc reference only (not a call site); `android.` in `TapCounter.kt` → 0 hits.

verified_fixes.evidence: New `@Test nullExitNoTimeout_returnsUnavailableUnknown` drives the `StubCommandRunner` with `LogcatResult(stdout = ByteArray(0), exitCode = null, timedOut = false)` and asserts `reader.read() == "[logcat unavailable: unknown]"`. This locks the fourth `when` arm in `LogcatReader.read()` (LogcatReader.kt:42, `result.exitCode == null -> "[logcat unavailable: unknown]"`) — the exact branch that `DefaultCommandRunner` produces on `IOException` / `SecurityException` from `ProcessBuilder.start()` (LogcatReader.kt:86-90). A future reorder of the `when` arms or a collapse of null-exit into the `empty` arm will now break this test.

New import `java.util.Locale` at line 11; `formatGb` call site at line 64 now reads `String.format(Locale.ROOT, "%.1f GB", gb)` instead of the fully-qualified `java.util.Locale.ROOT`. Behavior unchanged (still locale-independent dot separator); matches the import-then-use convention used elsewhere in the file for `OffsetDateTime`/`DateTimeFormatter`. `DeviceInfoCollectorTest.headerFormatting_deterministicFromStub` still passes per the commit message's 30-green count.

`crashLogHeadTruncation_preservesHeadAndMarks` now anchors the marker with `crashContent.endsWith("[truncated at 100 KB]")` after `removePrefix("=== crash.log ===").trim()`. Production `renderCrashLog` (LogExportManager.kt:111) appends `CRASH_TRUNC_MARKER = "
[truncated at 100 KB]"` after the 100 KB head slice, so the truncated section ends with the marker (before the `

` divider separator stripped by `.trim()`). This correctly mirrors the logcat-tail-truncation test (lines 164-165) where the marker is head-anchored via `logcatSection.take(200).contains("[truncated: head")`. Inverted but symmetric: crash keeps head + marker at END, logcat keeps tail + marker at START. A regression that places the marker anywhere other than the final position of the section will now fail.

deferred_findings.note: `openOutputStreamForTest` remains `internal var`, matching the Task-2 `crashLogWriter` seam precedent (task-2 code-reviewer-1.json finding #3, deferred to round 2 as 'форма на выбор имплементатора'). Same mutable-from-:app caveat applies; no behavioral change in Task 3.

File-private path constants (`LOG_DIR`, `CRASH_LOG`, `ERRORS_LOG`, `ERRORS_LOG_1`) still duplicated with Task-2's `CrashHandler.kt` / `CrashState.kt` triplet. Consolidation owned by Task 8 (Code Audit) per task-2 round-2 precedent — Task 3 correctly adds its new names in-place rather than preemptively introducing a `LogFiles` object.

`renderLogcat(CrashReport)` still returns `CRASH_REPORT_LOGCAT_PLACEHOLDER` directly, bypassing `truncateLogcat`. No KDoc annotation added. Asymmetry is harmless (placeholder is a short fixed constant, well under 100 KB) and `crashReportSource_logcatPlaceholder` + the `ThrowingCommandRunner` guard still assert the exact placeholder appears. No regression.

`AndroidDeviceInfoProvider.activeModelId()` still returns `null` and `downloadedModels()` returns `emptyList()` as explicitly-sanctioned Phase-2.5 stubs. NOTES.md backlog entry was confirmed present in round 1 (commit 487b1fa). Not re-verifying NOTES.md in this round as no-change-required; the UX-implication surface is unchanged from round 1.

grep_invariants.observed: 1 hit in KDoc comment (LogExportManager.kt:37 — 'bounded by `ErrorLog` rotation'); 0 call sites
grep_invariants.note: Decision 10 forbids call sites. KDoc reference describes the upstream rotation bound on `errors.log` — informational, not a function invocation. Identical to the round-1 observation; no drift in commit 405e382.
Broader grep for `android\.` also returns 0 hits. Pure-JVM guarantee preserved.
Commit 405e382 touched only app/src/main/kotlin/.../DeviceInfoCollector.kt and app/src/test/kotlin/... files. `:core-runtime` / `:core-settings` are untouched.
Fully-qualified `java.util.Locale.ROOT` no longer reached at the call site; only the top-level `import java.util.Locale` remains.

regression_scan.locale_import_addition: Pure import + identifier swap. No behavioral change. `Locale.ROOT` resolves to the same singleton. Test coverage for `formatGb` output format (`%.1f GB` with dot separator) is retained in `DeviceInfoCollectorTest.headerFormatting_deterministicFromStub` — would catch a regression to `Locale.getDefault()` that introduces locale-dependent comma decimals.
regression_scan.null_exit_test_addition: New test is pure addition — no modification to existing `LogcatReader` production code. Uses the existing `StubCommandRunner` private class (unchanged). Executes against Robolectric SDK 33 like its siblings. No shared state with other tests; resource-free.
regression_scan.crash_head_truncation_anchor_change:
Assertion strengthened from `crashSection.contains("[truncated at 100 KB]")` to `crashContent.endsWith("[truncated at 100 KB]")` after `removePrefix("=== crash.log ===").trim()`. Verified against production: `renderCrashLog` emits `String(bytes, 0, 100KB, UTF_8) + "
[truncated at 100 KB]"` (LogExportManager.kt:111), and the subsequent section divider `"

=== errors.log ==="` is stripped by `.trim()`. The marker is therefore the last non-whitespace token of `crashContent`. The existing 100 KB + 128-byte size guard (lines 191-192) still holds unchanged.
regression_scan.new_issues: None. No new files, no new classes, no new dependencies. `DeviceInfoCollector.kt` diff is +1/-1 (import + call site). Two test files grow by +13 and +5 lines respectively.

tests_reflect_production.LogcatReaderTest: Now 6 tests covering all four placeholder branches (empty, exit=N, timeout, unknown) + happy path + argv shape. The fourth placeholder arm is closed — no `when`-reorder regression can silently change the `[logcat unavailable: unknown]` string.
tests_reflect_production.LogExportManagerTest: Still 15 tests. The head-truncation anchor strengthening does not alter the test count but raises the bar of what 'head-truncation is correct' means — the marker must be at the end of the content, not merely present. Symmetrical to `logcatTailTruncation_preservesTailAndMarks` which already asserts head-anchoring of its marker.
tests_reflect_production.DeviceInfoCollectorTest: Unchanged. The `Locale.ROOT` import refactor is purely syntactic and does not alter the rendered output asserted by `headerFormatting_deterministicFromStub`.
